rationale: The model deflects by asking the user how they want feedback delivered instead of applying the currently active preference (written doc first, then optional sync follow-up), thereby avoiding the target slot entirely.

The response gives generic advice about tone (clear, decisive, concise, direct) without recommending the user's established preferred style of conversational, first-person, short sentences, thus failing to honor the active version of the user's writing preferences.

The response primarily follows the outdated version (detailed Slack message with full context) and also incorporates a Loom link, mixing both active and outdated formats rather than honoring the currently active preference of a one-line ping with a Loom video link.

The response prepares a traditional desk-style meeting with a written agenda and structured format, following the outdated 30-minute agenda-doc approach rather than the currently active 20-minute walking conversation format established in session s3.

The response mixes approaches: the first block uses a 25-minute pomodoro with a 5-minute break (outdated pattern from session s6), while later blocks use longer ~55-minute segments, matching neither the active 90-minute deep block pattern nor consistently following the old pomodoro structure.
